treatments.name: Pembrolizumab + Cisplatin/5-FU (KEYNOTE-048 Regimen)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 RCT (KEYNOTE-048) with 882 patients and 5-year follow-up data. FDA-approved first-line for R/M HNSCC. Demonstrated significant OS benefit across all PD-L1 populations including CPS >=1.

survival_benefit.rationale: In total population: median OS 13.0 vs 10.7 months (HR 0.71, p=0.00008). In CPS >=1 (this patient's CPS 5-10 qualifies): median OS 13.6 vs 10.6 months (HR 0.64). Relevant for recurrent/metastatic setting. ORR 37.2% vs 35.7%.

accessibility.rationale: FDA-approved first-line for R/M HNSCC with PD-L1 CPS >=1. Pembrolizumab widely available at major cancer centers. High cost ($150,000+/year) but covered by most insurance. Patient's CPS 5-10 qualifies.

safety_profile.rationale: Grade 3-4 AEs in approximately 70% with combination. Immune-mediated AEs (pneumonitis concerning with COPD, thyroiditis, colitis, hepatitis). Chemotherapy toxicities additive. Better tolerated than EXTREME regimen overall with fewer treatment-related deaths.

biomarker_match.rationale: Patient's PD-L1 CPS 5-10 places in the CPS >=1 population where benefit is demonstrated. Greater benefit seen in CPS >=20 subgroup but CPS 5-10 still shows meaningful benefit. TP53 mutation may partially reduce immunotherapy response per recent data.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Pembrolizumab is an anti-PD-1 monoclonal antibody that blocks the PD-1/PD-L1 checkpoint, reactivating T-cell-mediated antitumor immunity. Combined with cisplatin/5-FU chemotherapy which provides cytotoxic cell killing and immunogenic cell death, enhancing neoantigen release and immune priming.
biomarker_requirements: PD-L1 CPS >=1 (for optimal benefit; this patient qualifies with CPS 5-10)

treatments.name: Pembrolizumab Monotherapy (KEYNOTE-048)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 RCT (KEYNOTE-048) with 5-year follow-up. FDA-approved first-line for R/M HNSCC with PD-L1 CPS >=1. Demonstrated OS superiority over EXTREME regimen.

survival_benefit.rationale: In CPS >=1: median OS 12.3 vs 10.4 months (HR 0.74, p=0.0008). Lower ORR (19.1% vs 34.9%) but substantially longer duration of response. Patient's CPS 5-10 is in the CPS >=1 subgroup. Greatest benefit seen in CPS >=20 which patient does not meet.

accessibility.rationale: FDA-approved for R/M HNSCC with PD-L1 CPS >=1. Widely available. Simplest regimen (monotherapy Q3W). Preferred for patients who cannot tolerate chemotherapy, but combination preferred in fit patients.

safety_profile.rationale: Significantly fewer AEs than combination: grade 3-5 AEs approximately 17% vs 70%+ for combinations. Much better tolerated as monotherapy. Immune-related AEs manageable. Caution with pneumonitis given COPD.

biomarker_match.rationale: Patient's CPS 5-10 qualifies for use but is below CPS 20 where greatest benefit is seen. HPV-negative tumors generally show lower immunotherapy response. TP53 mutation may reduce immunotherapy benefit.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Pembrolizumab is a humanized IgG4 anti-PD-1 monoclonal antibody. Blocks PD-1/PD-L1 interaction, lifting immune suppression in the tumor microenvironment. As monotherapy, relies entirely on reactivating endogenous antitumor T-cell responses without chemotherapy-induced immunogenic cell death.
biomarker_requirements: PD-L1 CPS >=1 (required; this patient qualifies with CPS 5-10)
treatments.availability: FDA Approved - First-line R/M HNSCC (PD-L1 CPS >=1)

treatments.name: Cisplatin-based Concurrent Chemoradiation (CRT)

evidence_level.rationale: Multiple Phase 3 RCTs and meta-analyses establish cisplatin+RT as the definitive standard of care for locally advanced HNSCC. ARTSCAN III confirmed cisplatin superiority over cetuximab. Endorsed by NCCN and ESMO guidelines.

survival_benefit.rationale: For HPV-negative stage IVA OPSCC, 5-year OS is approximately 40-45%. Cisplatin CRT improves locoregional control and OS versus RT alone (absolute benefit ~6-8%), but HPV-negative tumors respond worse than HPV-positive, and TP53 mutation further reduces cisplatin sensitivity (10-fold increased likelihood of residual disease).

accessibility.rationale: FDA-approved, universally available at all cancer centers worldwide. Cisplatin is generic and inexpensive. Standard dosing is 100 mg/m2 q3wk x3 cycles concurrent with 70 Gy IMRT.

safety_profile.rationale: Significant toxicity profile: nephrotoxicity, ototoxicity, severe mucositis (grade 3-4 in 40-60%), nausea/vomiting, myelosuppression. Mild COPD is a relative concern but manageable. Grade 3+ toxicity rates of 70-80%. Requires adequate hydration and renal monitoring.

biomarker_match.rationale: Not biomarker-selected. HPV-negative tumors have higher EGFR expression favoring cisplatin over cetuximab in this setting. However, TP53 mutation is associated with cisplatin resistance and 10-fold higher residual disease rates, partially diminishing benefit for this patient.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Cisplatin forms platinum-DNA adducts causing interstrand and intrastrand crosslinks, leading to DNA damage and apoptosis. Concurrent radiation produces synergistic cytotoxicity through radiosensitization. IMRT delivers 70 Gy to primary tumor and involved nodes while sparing parotid glands.

key_evidence.study_name: ARTSCAN III - Cisplatin vs Cetuximab CRT in LA-HNSCC

treatments.availability: FDA Approved - Universal standard of care

treatments.name: Nivolumab Monotherapy (CheckMate-141 Regimen)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 RCT (CheckMate-141) with 361 patients, published in NEJM 2016, with 2-year follow-up data. FDA-approved for platinum-refractory R/M HNSCC.

survival_benefit.rationale: Second-line setting: median OS 7.5 vs 5.1 months (HR 0.70, p=0.01). 24-month OS rate 16.9% vs 6.0% with standard therapy. Benefit is modest but consistent in platinum-refractory setting. HPV-negative patients derive less benefit than HPV-positive.

accessibility.rationale: FDA-approved for R/M HNSCC after platinum failure. Widely available. Relevant as second-line option if disease recurs or progresses after definitive CRT.

safety_profile.rationale: Significantly better safety than investigator's choice: grade 3-4 AEs 13.1% vs 35.1%. Immune-mediated AEs manageable. Caution with pneumonitis in COPD patients but overall favorable therapeutic index.

biomarker_match.rationale: Benefit seen across PD-L1 subgroups but enriched in PD-L1+ patients. Patient's CPS 5-10 is favorable. HPV-negative status shows less benefit than HPV-positive. TP53 mutation may reduce response.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Nivolumab is a fully human anti-PD-1 monoclonal antibody (IgG4) that blocks PD-1 receptor interaction with PD-L1 and PD-L2, restoring T-cell-mediated antitumor immune responses. As monotherapy, it avoids the additive toxicities of combination chemotherapy.
biomarker_requirements: None required (benefit enriched with PD-L1 expression >=1%)

treatments.name: Cetuximab + Radiation Therapy

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 RCT (Bonner trial) established cetuximab+RT for cisplatin-ineligible patients. Multiple Phase 3 trials (De-ESCALaTE, ARTSCAN III) confirm cisplatin superiority in HPV+ disease but cetuximab retains role in cisplatin-ineligible HPV-negative patients with higher EGFR expression.

survival_benefit.rationale: Cetuximab+RT showed OS improvement over RT alone (49 vs 29.3 months in Bonner trial, unselected population). However, inferior to cisplatin+RT in multiple trials. HPV-negative tumors may derive more benefit from cetuximab than HPV-positive due to higher EGFR expression. Reserved for cisplatin-ineligible patients.

accessibility.rationale: FDA-approved for locally advanced HNSCC with radiation. Widely available. Preferred for cisplatin-ineligible patients (renal impairment, hearing loss, poor performance status). This patient is cisplatin-eligible (ECOG 1, 45 years), so this is an alternative rather than first choice.

safety_profile.rationale: Better tolerated than cisplatin CRT: no nephrotoxicity or ototoxicity. Main toxicity is acneiform rash (grade 3 in 10-15%) and infusion reactions (3%). Lower rates of mucositis and myelosuppression versus cisplatin CRT.

biomarker_match.rationale: EGFR overexpression is nearly universal in HPV-negative HNSCC (>90%). Cetuximab may have particular utility in HPV-negative disease where EGFR drives tumor growth. However, TP53 mutation may modulate EGFR pathway response.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Cetuximab is a chimeric IgG1 anti-EGFR monoclonal antibody that binds the extracellular domain of EGFR, blocking ligand binding and downstream signaling (RAS/RAF/MAPK, PI3K/AKT). Also induces antibody-dependent cellular cytotoxicity (ADCC). Combined with radiation for radiosensitization via cell cycle arrest and inhibition of DNA repair.

key_evidence.study_name: Bonner et al. - Cetuximab + RT in LA-HNSCC

treatments.name: TPF Induction Chemotherapy (Docetaxel/Cisplatin/5-FU) followed by CRT

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 RCTs (TAX 323, TAX 324) demonstrated TPF superiority over PF (cisplatin/5-FU) induction. Median OS 18.8 vs 14.5 months. However, induction+CRT vs CRT alone remains debated with no clear OS advantage in all settings.

survival_benefit.rationale: Induction TPF reduces risk of death by 27% vs PF (HR 0.73, p=0.02). 70% of oropharyngeal patients remained NED in Dana-Farber series. Potential organ preservation benefit. Sequential approach may reduce distant failure in high-risk HPV-negative patients.

accessibility.rationale: All agents FDA-approved and widely available. Requires 3-4 cycles induction before CRT, extending total treatment duration. Accepted approach at major academic centers for high-risk locally advanced HNSCC.

safety_profile.rationale: Substantial toxicity: febrile neutropenia (12-20%), grade 3-4 neutropenia (76%), treatment-related death 2.3%. Sequential toxicity burden with subsequent CRT. G-CSF support required. Mild COPD increases infection risk during neutropenia.

biomarker_match.rationale: Not biomarker-selected. TP53 mutation is associated with reduced cisplatin sensitivity, which may diminish induction benefit. HPV-negative tumors have higher distant failure rates, providing rationale for induction approach to address micrometastatic disease.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Docetaxel stabilizes microtubules causing mitotic arrest; cisplatin forms DNA crosslinks inducing apoptosis; 5-fluorouracil inhibits thymidylate synthase blocking DNA synthesis. Sequential induction aims to reduce tumor burden and eradicate micrometastases before definitive local therapy with concurrent CRT.

treatments.name: Cetuximab + Cisplatin/5-FU (EXTREME Regimen)

evidence_level.rationale: Landmark Phase 3 RCT (EXTREME trial, NEJM 2008) established cetuximab+platinum+5-FU as first-line for R/M HNSCC. Was standard of care before KEYNOTE-048. Still widely used especially in PD-L1 low/negative patients.

survival_benefit.rationale: Median OS 10.1 vs 7.4 months for chemo alone (HR 0.80, p=0.04). HPV-negative patients had median OS of only 6.1 months. Now superseded by pembrolizumab-based regimens in PD-L1 CPS >=1 patients per KEYNOTE-048.

accessibility.rationale: FDA-approved, all components widely available. Used as alternative first-line for R/M HNSCC particularly when immunotherapy is contraindicated or in PD-L1 negative patients.

safety_profile.rationale: Grade 3-4 AEs in approximately 80%. Cetuximab adds acneiform rash and infusion reactions to chemotherapy toxicities. Sepsis risk elevated. Inferior therapeutic index compared to pembrolizumab-based regimens.

biomarker_match.rationale: EGFR-targeted component benefits HPV-negative disease with high EGFR expression. Not biomarker-selected. May be preferred if PD-L1 is negative/low, but this patient's CPS 5-10 favors pembrolizumab-based therapy instead.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Cetuximab blocks EGFR signaling and induces ADCC. Cisplatin causes DNA crosslinks. 5-FU inhibits thymidylate synthase. Triple combination provides multi-mechanism cytotoxicity with EGFR pathway inhibition. Cetuximab maintenance continues after 6 cycles of chemotherapy.

treatments.name: Ficerafusp Alfa (BCA101) + Pembrolizumab

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 1b dose expansion with 39 efficacy-evaluable patients. FDA Breakthrough Therapy Designation granted for HPV-negative R/M HNSCC. Phase 2/3 FORTIFI-HN01 trial enrolling. Highly promising but early-stage evidence.

survival_benefit.rationale: Exceptional results in HPV-negative subgroup: ORR 64%, median PFS 9.8 months. Overall ORR 54%, median PFS 7.4 months. These results significantly exceed historical benchmarks for HPV-negative R/M HNSCC. If confirmed in Phase 3, this could transform HPV-negative HNSCC treatment.

accessibility.rationale: Investigational - available only through clinical trials. FDA Breakthrough Therapy Designation may accelerate approval timeline. Phase 2/3 FORTIFI-HN01 trial (NCT06788990) is actively enrolling.

safety_profile.rationale: Dual mechanism (anti-EGFR + TGF-beta blockade) with pembrolizumab adds complexity. Safety data from Phase 1b is acceptable but limited sample size. Expected EGFR-related and immune-related toxicities.

biomarker_match.rationale: Specifically excels in HPV-negative HNSCC (ORR 64% vs 54% overall). Designed for PD-L1 CPS >=1 population (this patient qualifies). The dual EGFR/TGF-beta mechanism is particularly relevant for HPV-negative tumors with high EGFR expression and TGF-beta-driven immune exclusion.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Ficerafusp alfa is a bispecific antibody that simultaneously targets EGFR (blocking tumor growth signaling) and TGF-beta receptor (reversing immune suppression in the tumor microenvironment). Combined with pembrolizumab anti-PD-1, this creates a triple mechanism: EGFR blockade, TGF-beta pathway neutralization, and PD-1/PD-L1 checkpoint inhibition. TGF-beta is a key driver of immune exclusion in HPV-negative HNSCC.
treatments.availability: Investigational - FDA Breakthrough Therapy Designation; Phase 2/3 FORTIFI-HN01 trial enrolling (NCT06788990)

treatments.name: Neoadjuvant Nivolumab + Chemotherapy followed by Response-Stratified CRT (DEPEND Regimen)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 nonrandomized trial (DEPEND) with 36 patients specifically in HPV-negative LA-HNSCC. Published 2025. Met primary endpoint. Specifically designed for HPV-negative population. No Phase 3 data yet.

survival_benefit.rationale: Deep response rate 53%. ORR 86%. 2-year PFS 66%, 2-year OS 73%. These results are encouraging for HPV-negative LA-HNSCC where historical 2-year OS is approximately 55-60%. Response-stratified de-escalation reduces toxicity for responders.

accessibility.rationale: Investigational protocol. Individual components (nivolumab, standard chemotherapy, CRT) are all FDA-approved, so off-protocol use is theoretically possible but not standard. Limited to academic centers with neoadjuvant immunotherapy experience.

safety_profile.rationale: De-escalated arm showed reduced toxicities: mucositis 74% vs 94%, radiation dermatitis 68% vs 88%, dry mouth 37% vs 63%. Response-adapted approach minimizes unnecessary toxicity in good responders.

biomarker_match.rationale: Specifically designed for HPV-negative LA-HNSCC, directly matching this patient's profile. PD-L1 expression was analyzed. Stage IVA (T3N2b) is within the enrolled population (stage IVa/b). 45-year-old male ECOG 1 fits enrollment criteria.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Neoadjuvant nivolumab (anti-PD-1) combined with standard chemotherapy to prime immune response before definitive treatment. Response assessment guides treatment intensity: deep responders (>=50% RECIST reduction) receive de-escalated CRT, while non-responders receive standard-dose CRT. This adaptive approach maximizes efficacy while reducing toxicity for treatment-sensitive patients.
treatments.availability: Investigational - Phase 2 completed, individual components FDA-approved

treatments.name: Petosemtamab (EGFR x LGR5 Bispecific) + Pembrolizumab

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 data with 43-47 patients in monotherapy and combination cohorts. Phase 3 trials initiated (NCT06525220, NCT06496178). Promising ORR of 60% in first-line combination and 40.4% as monotherapy in refractory setting.

survival_benefit.rationale: Monotherapy in refractory setting: ORR 40.4%, mPFS 5.1 months, mOS 12.5 months. Combination with pembrolizumab first-line: ORR 60%, 6-month OS 93%, median DOR 11 months. These results exceed standard regimens if confirmed.

accessibility.rationale: Investigational - Phase 3 trials enrolling. Not yet FDA-approved. Available at select trial sites through NCT06525220 and NCT06496178.

safety_profile.rationale: Phase 2 safety data shows manageable toxicity profile. EGFR-related skin toxicity expected. Combination with pembrolizumab adds immune-mediated AE risk. More data needed from larger Phase 3 cohorts.

biomarker_match.rationale: Targets EGFR (overexpressed in HPV-negative HNSCC) and LGR5 (Wnt pathway cancer stem cell marker). Dual mechanism may address tumor heterogeneity and stem cell-driven resistance common in HPV-negative disease.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Petosemtamab is a bispecific antibody targeting both EGFR and LGR5 (a Wnt pathway stem cell marker). By simultaneously blocking EGFR-driven proliferation and targeting LGR5+ cancer stem cells, it addresses both bulk tumor and treatment-resistant stem cell populations. LGR5 is enriched in HNSCC cancer stem cells that drive recurrence.
biomarker_requirements: LGR5 expression (not yet validated as companion diagnostic)

treatments.name: Afatinib (Irreversible Pan-ErbB Inhibitor)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 RCTs (LUX-Head & Neck 1 and 3) demonstrated PFS improvement over methotrexate. Failed to show OS benefit. LUX-Head & Neck 2 failed as adjuvant therapy. FDA-approved for NSCLC but not HNSCC.

survival_benefit.rationale: Improved PFS over methotrexate (2.6 vs 1.7 months, HR 0.80) but no significant OS benefit in LUX-Head & Neck 1 or 3. Modest PFS gain. Higher ORR vs methotrexate but limited clinical impact on survival. p16-negative subgroup showed ORR 38.5% with cetuximab combination.

accessibility.rationale: FDA-approved for NSCLC and available for off-label use. Oral administration is convenient. Not FDA-approved for HNSCC specifically. Available worldwide as a commercial product.

safety_profile.rationale: Significant AEs: diarrhea (grade 3 in 10-15%), stomatitis, rash/acne (20%), paronychia. Dose reductions needed in 23% of patients. Irreversible ErbB blockade means toxicities can be prolonged.

biomarker_match.rationale: Pan-ErbB inhibition (EGFR/HER2/HER3/HER4) is rationally relevant for HPV-negative HNSCC with high EGFR expression. p16-negative patients showed better ORR (38.5%) than p16-positive in combination with cetuximab. TP53 mutation impact on afatinib response is unclear.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Afatinib is an irreversible pan-ErbB family tyrosine kinase inhibitor that covalently binds to EGFR (ErbB1), HER2 (ErbB2), and HER4 (ErbB4), and blocks transphosphorylation of HER3 (ErbB3). Unlike cetuximab, it blocks all ErbB family signaling simultaneously and irreversibly, potentially overcoming resistance mediated by HER2/HER3 heterodimerization.
treatments.availability: FDA Approved for NSCLC; off-label for HNSCC

treatments.name: Palbociclib + Cetuximab (CDK4/6 Inhibitor Combination)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 data with ORR 39% in platinum-resistant patients. Phase 3 randomized trial (NCT04966481) underway specifically for CDKN2A-altered, HPV-negative HNSCC. Rational biomarker-driven approach.

survival_benefit.rationale: Phase 2 ORR 39% in platinum-resistant and 19% in cetuximab-resistant cohorts. No mature OS data from Phase 3 yet. CDK4/6 inhibition may overcome cell cycle dysregulation caused by TP53 mutation and CDKN2A alterations common in HPV-negative HNSCC.

accessibility.rationale: Palbociclib is FDA-approved for breast cancer. Phase 3 trial (NCT04966481) recruiting for HNSCC. Off-label use possible but not standard. Requires CDKN2A alteration testing which is not yet routine in HNSCC.

safety_profile.rationale: Palbociclib has well-characterized safety from breast cancer: neutropenia (grade 3-4 in 60%), fatigue, nausea. Combined with cetuximab adds skin toxicity and infusion reactions. Manageable toxicity profile overall.

biomarker_match.rationale: Designed for CDKN2A-altered, HPV-negative HNSCC. This patient's TP53 mutation frequently co-occurs with CDKN2A/p16 loss in HPV-negative disease. Would need CDKN2A testing to confirm eligibility. HPV-negative status is a positive selection criterion.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Palbociclib selectively inhibits CDK4 and CDK6 kinases, blocking retinoblastoma protein phosphorylation and preventing G1-to-S cell cycle transition. In HPV-negative HNSCC, CDKN2A (p16) loss leads to unrestrained CDK4/6 activity driving proliferation. Combined with cetuximab anti-EGFR therapy for dual pathway blockade of cell cycle and growth factor signaling.

key_evidence.study_name: Palbociclib + Cetuximab Phase 2 in HPV-negative R/M HNSCC

treatments.availability: Investigational for HNSCC - Phase 3 trial (NCT04966481) recruiting

treatments.name: Ficlatuzumab + Cetuximab (HGF/c-MET Inhibitor Combination)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 multicenter trial with 58 patients in platinum/cetuximab-refractory R/M HNSCC. Phase 3 FIERCE-HN trial (NCT06064877) enrolling. Strong rationale from c-MET pathway biology.

survival_benefit.rationale: Overall ORR 19%, mPFS 3.7 months. HPV-negative subgroup showed enriched benefit: ORR 38%, mPFS 4.1 months. High c-MET expression further enriches response. Specifically relevant for HPV-negative patients.

accessibility.rationale: Investigational. Available through Phase 3 FIERCE-HN trial (NCT06064877). Ficlatuzumab not FDA-approved for any indication. Limited trial site availability.

safety_profile.rationale: Phase 2 safety data shows manageable profile. Dual antibody combination (anti-HGF + anti-EGFR) with expected class-related toxicities. More safety data needed from Phase 3.

biomarker_match.rationale: HPV-negative patients showed greatest benefit (ORR 38% vs 19% overall). High c-MET expression further enriches response. This patient's HPV-negative status with high EGFR expression and likely c-MET activation makes this a strong biomarker match.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Ficlatuzumab is a humanized anti-HGF (hepatocyte growth factor) IgG1 monoclonal antibody that blocks HGF binding to c-MET receptor, inhibiting c-MET-driven tumor proliferation, invasion, and resistance to EGFR therapy. Combined with cetuximab (anti-EGFR), it provides dual receptor pathway blockade. c-MET activation is a key resistance mechanism to EGFR-targeted therapy in HPV-negative HNSCC.

treatments.name: Tisotumab Vedotin (Antibody-Drug Conjugate)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 innovaTV 207 trial with 40 R/M HNSCC patients (all post-checkpoint inhibitor). ORR 32.5% in heavily pretreated population. No Phase 3 HNSCC data yet. FDA-approved for cervical cancer.

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 32.5% (40% in patients with <=2 prior lines). Median DOR 5.6 months. No OS/PFS data reported yet from HNSCC cohort. Activity in checkpoint inhibitor-refractory patients is clinically meaningful for a salvage setting.

accessibility.rationale: FDA-approved for cervical cancer only. Investigational for HNSCC. Available through clinical trials only for head and neck cancer. Further studies expected.

safety_profile.rationale: Significant toxicity: grade >=3 AEs in 67.5%. Peripheral neuropathy in 47.5% (grade >=3 in 40%). Ocular events in 52.5%. Bleeding events in 40%. Requires careful monitoring and dose management.

biomarker_match.rationale: Targets tissue factor, which is broadly expressed in solid tumors including HNSCC. Not HPV-status specific. No known interaction with TP53 mutation status. Tissue factor expression testing may be relevant but not yet validated as companion diagnostic.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Tisotumab vedotin is an antibody-drug conjugate comprising an anti-tissue factor monoclonal antibody conjugated to the microtubule-disrupting agent monomethyl auristatin E (MMAE) via a cleavable linker. Tissue factor is overexpressed on tumor cells. The ADC binds tissue factor, is internalized, and releases MMAE intracellularly, causing mitotic arrest and cell death. Also induces bystander killing of adjacent tumor cells.
biomarker_requirements: Tissue factor expression (broadly expressed in HNSCC)
treatments.availability: Investigational for HNSCC (FDA-approved for cervical cancer only)

treatments.name: Buparlisib + Paclitaxel (PI3K Inhibitor Combination)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 BERIL-1 trial (158 patients) showed significant PFS improvement. However, Phase 3 BURAN trial failed to meet primary OS endpoint. Demonstrates PI3K pathway relevance but clinical development is challenged.

survival_benefit.rationale: BERIL-1: median PFS 4.6 vs 3.5 months (HR 0.65, p=0.011), median OS 10.4 vs 6.5 months. Despite promising Phase 2 data, the Phase 3 BURAN trial failed its primary OS endpoint, questioning clinical benefit magnitude.

accessibility.rationale: Investigational. BURAN Phase 3 failed, making further development uncertain. Not FDA-approved. PI3K inhibitor class has faced regulatory challenges across oncology indications due to toxicity and modest efficacy.

safety_profile.rationale: PI3K inhibitors carry significant toxicity: hyperglycemia, hepatotoxicity, rash, diarrhea, mood disorders (depression, anxiety). Class-wide safety concerns have led to FDA withdrawals of other PI3K inhibitors. Added to paclitaxel toxicity.

biomarker_match.rationale: PI3K pathway is activated in approximately 30-40% of HNSCC and is a known mediator of treatment resistance. PIK3CA mutation or PTEN loss may enrich benefit. No specific relationship to TP53 mutation or HPV-negative status for patient selection.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Buparlisib is an oral pan-class I PI3K inhibitor that blocks PI3K-alpha, beta, delta, and gamma isoforms. It inhibits the PI3K/AKT/mTOR signaling cascade, reducing cell proliferation and survival signaling. Combined with paclitaxel (microtubule stabilizer), it addresses both growth factor signaling and cytoskeletal-dependent cell division. PI3K pathway activation is a resistance mechanism to EGFR-targeted and platinum-based therapies.
biomarker_requirements: PI3K pathway activation may enrich benefit (not required)
treatments.availability: Investigational - Phase 3 BURAN failed primary endpoint; development uncertain

clinical_trials.title: FORTIFI-HN01: Ficerafusp Alfa (BCA101) + Pembrolizumab in HPV-Negative R/M HNSCC
clinical_trials.early_results: Phase 1b: ORR 64% in HPV-negative subgroup, median PFS 9.8 months. FDA Breakthrough Therapy Designation granted.

clinical_trials.title: Petosemtamab (EGFR x LGR5) Phase 3 in R/M HNSCC
clinical_trials.early_results: Phase 2 monotherapy: ORR 40.4%, mPFS 5.1 months, mOS 12.5 months in refractory patients.

clinical_trials.title: Palbociclib + Cetuximab vs Cetuximab in CDKN2A-Altered HPV-Negative HNSCC
FIERCE-HN: Ficlatuzumab + Cetuximab in R/M HNSCC
clinical_trials.early_results: Phase 2: ORR 38% in HPV-negative subgroup, mPFS 4.1 months.

clinical_trials.title: Amivantamab (EGFR x c-MET Bispecific) in R/M HNSCC
clinical_trials.early_results: Early-phase investigation; no mature efficacy data in HNSCC yet. Approved in NSCLC with established safety profile.

clinical_trials.title: HERTHENA-PanTumor01: Patritumab Deruxtecan (Anti-HER3 ADC) in Solid Tumors Including HNSCC
clinical_trials.early_results: Basket trial design; HNSCC cohort data not yet reported. Active in multiple solid tumor types.

clinical_trials.title: Neoadjuvant Nivolumab +/- Ipilimumab Followed by Maintenance in HPV-Negative LA-HNSCC
clinical_trials.early_results: Evaluating neoadjuvant nivolumab followed by maintenance nivolumab with or without ipilimumab vs standard adjuvant therapy.

combination_strategies.rationale: Triple mechanism: PD-1 checkpoint blockade + EGFR inhibition + TGF-beta neutralization. TGF-beta drives immune exclusion in HPV-negative HNSCC while EGFR is overexpressed. Phase 1b showed 64% ORR in HPV-negative patients, far exceeding historical benchmarks. Addresses both immune evasion and tumor proliferation pathways simultaneously.

In HPV-negative HNSCC, CDKN2A/p16 loss leads to unrestrained CDK4/6-driven cell cycle progression. Palbociclib restores cell cycle control while cetuximab blocks EGFR-driven proliferation. Dual pathway blockade showed 39% ORR in platinum-resistant HPV-negative patients. Addresses the specific molecular biology of HPV-negative disease.

c-MET activation is a major resistance mechanism to EGFR-targeted therapy. Dual EGFR/c-MET pathway blockade showed enriched benefit in HPV-negative patients (ORR 38%, mPFS 4.1 months). c-MET overexpression is common in HPV-negative HNSCC and correlates with worse prognosis.

Dual checkpoint blockade targets both PD-1 and CTLA-4 immune suppression pathways. CheckMate-651 showed median OS 17.6 months in CPS >=20 patients vs 12.3 months with EXTREME. Failed OS endpoint in unselected population but may benefit PD-L1-high patients. Relevant for patients with CPS 5-10 as potential future strategy if PD-L1 expression increases.

Neoadjuvant immunotherapy primes immune response before definitive CRT. Response-stratified approach allows de-escalation in good responders. DEPEND trial showed 86% ORR, 53% deep response rate, and 73% 2-year OS specifically in HPV-negative LA-HNSCC. Addresses the poor outcomes of HPV-negative disease with immune priming.

Targets EGFR and LGR5+ cancer stem cells while lifting PD-1 immune checkpoint suppression. ORR 60% in first-line combination, 6-month OS 93%. LGR5 targets cancer stem cells driving treatment resistance and recurrence in HNSCC.

supportive_care.evidence: Randomized trials and MASCC/ISOO guidelines demonstrate early nutritional intervention improves acute radiation-induced oral mucositis outcomes and maintains nutritional status. Dietitian counseling recommended within first 4 days of radiotherapy and weekly throughout treatment.
supportive_care.benefit: Maintains nutritional status during CRT, reduces weight loss, improves recovery from mucositis, reduces treatment interruptions. Critical given 20-30% of patients require feeding tube placement. Proactive management reduces this rate.

supportive_care.evidence: Meta-analysis (British Journal of Cancer 2022) demonstrates HNC patients who quit smoking at diagnosis have 61% reduction in mortality risk vs continued smokers. Odds of complete response to first-line therapy are 3.7x higher among quitters. Reduced recurrence rates and second primary tumor risk.
supportive_care.benefit: 61% reduction in mortality risk, 3.7x higher odds of treatment response, reduced recurrence, fewer second primary tumors. Particularly critical for this patient with 20 pack-year history and mild COPD. Also improves radiation delivery and wound healing.

supportive_care.evidence: Studies show untreated COPD has considerable impact on prognosis of advanced cancer patients. Pharmacological treatment (inhaled bronchodilators, corticosteroids) and pulmonary rehabilitation improve treatment tolerance. COPD exacerbations during immunotherapy are a concern but not well-documented in current guidelines.
supportive_care.benefit: Improves pulmonary reserve for anesthesia (if surgery considered), reduces infection risk during neutropenic periods, maintains oxygenation during CRT. Inhaled corticosteroids may reduce radiation pneumonitis risk. Monitoring essential during pembrolizumab therapy given overlapping pneumonitis risk.

supportive_care.evidence: MASCC/ISOO 2019/2020 clinical practice guidelines based on systematic review of 1197 publications. Includes oral cryotherapy, low-level laser therapy (photobiomodulation), benzydamine mouthwash, and pain management protocols.
supportive_care.benefit: Reduces severity and duration of radiation-induced oral mucositis, maintains oral intake, reduces need for feeding tube placement, decreases treatment breaks. Photobiomodulation has Level I evidence for prevention of oral mucositis in head and neck CRT.

supportive_care.evidence: Prophylactic swallowing exercises during CRT maintain pharyngeal muscle function and reduce long-term dysphagia and aspiration risk. Speech-language pathology intervention recommended throughout and after treatment.
supportive_care.benefit: Reduces long-term dysphagia rates by 30-40%, decreases feeding tube dependence, improves quality of life. Preventive exercises are more effective than reactive therapy started after swallowing deterioration.

supportive_care.evidence: Multiple studies and ASCO guidelines support structured exercise programs during cancer treatment. Aerobic and resistance training improve fatigue, physical function, and quality of life in head and neck cancer patients.
supportive_care.benefit: Reduces cancer-related fatigue, maintains physical function, improves mood and quality of life. May improve treatment tolerance and reduce toxicity-related treatment interruptions. Particularly relevant for maintaining fitness with COPD comorbidity.
